rawContent: {"stage":"review","insights":[{"kind":"decision","text":"Using a dedicated `course-instructors` collection (not embedded array) for per-course role assignments enables Payload access control and querying per course.","scope":"auth/permissions"},{"kind":"decision","text":"The `primary`/`ta`/`guest` trinary permission model on course-instructors provides sufficient granularity for instructor hierarchy without overcomplicating the role system.","scope":"auth/permissions"},{"kind":"decision","text":"Branching the dashboard API handler on user role (admin vs instructor) keeps the endpoint unified while enabling role-specific logic and data shaping.","scope":"src/server/api"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"UI rendering logic must be verified in-browser — heading text (e.g. 'Course Oversight') and conditional rendering depend on role context not always visible in unit tests.","scope":"E2E testing"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Browser verification of role-dependent UI text catches discrepancies that un
